{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8597","id":2521892372,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFKgkM6WUQYU","number":8597,"title":"Support for NEP-13 ","user":{"login":"ianna","id":1390682,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzOTA2ODI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1390682?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna","html_url":"https://github.com/ianna","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":545923268,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw1NDU5MjMyNjg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/cat:feature","name":"cat:feature","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"New features/APIs"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-12T09:43:54Z","updated_at":"2024-11-07T16:47:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

It would be nice to have the support for cupy ufuncs on the array-like objects that define an `__array_ufunc__` method ([NEP-13](https://numpy.org/neps/nep-0013-ufunc-overrides.html))

Awkward knows how to dispatch through numpy ufuncs, and will dispatch to cupy ufuncs when encountering the "cuda" backend.

However, when using cupy ufuncs on awkward arrays with cuda backing it doesn't work out of the box!

Please, see the original discussion and a reproducer are here:
https://github.com/scikit-hep/awkward/issues/3232

### Additional Information

also related to it:
https://github.com/NVIDIA/numba-cuda/issues/37

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8597/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8597","id":2521892372,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFKgkM6WUQYU","number":8597,"title":"Support for NEP-13 ","user":{"login":"ianna","id":1390682,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzOTA2ODI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1390682?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna","html_url":"https://github.com/ianna","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianna/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":545923268,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw1NDU5MjMyNjg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/cat:feature","name":"cat:feature","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"New features/APIs"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-12T09:43:54Z","updated_at":"2024-09-12T09:43:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

It would be nice to have the support for cupy ufuncs on the array-like objects that define an `__array_ufunc__` method ([NEP-13](https://numpy.org/neps/nep-0013-ufunc-overrides.html))

Awkward knows how to dispatch through numpy ufuncs, and will dispatch to cupy ufuncs when encountering the "cuda" backend.

However, when using cupy ufuncs on awkward arrays with cuda backing it doesn't work out of the box!

Please, see the original discussion and a reproducer are here:
https://github.com/scikit-hep/awkward/issues/3232

also related to it:
https://github.com/NVIDIA/numba-cuda/issues/37

### Additional Information

Mac","user":{"login":"mharradon","id":308108,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODEwOA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/308108?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon","html_url":"https://github.com/mharradon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mharradon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":671605061,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2NzE2MDUwNjE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/julia-feedstock/labels/bug","name":"bug","color":"ee0701","default":true,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-03-27T23:45:26Z","updated_at":"2024-06-06T14:24:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Solution to issue cannot be found in the documentation.

- [X] I checked the documentation.

### Issue

Installing Julia just from conda-forge onto an Intel Mac results in CHOLMOD warning. I am not sure if what it states is true, as apparently this warning has been erroneous in the past:

```
» conda create --override-channels --channel conda-forge -n julia-test julia
…
» conda activate julia-test
» julia --project=.
               _
   _       _ _(_)_     |  Documentation: https://docs.julialang.org
  (_)     | (_) (_)    |
   _ _   _| |_  __ _   |  Type "?" for help, "]?" for Pkg help.
  | | | | | | |/ _` |  |
  | | |_| | | | (_| |  |  Version 1.10.2 (2024-03-01)
 _/ |\__'_|_|_|\__'_|  |  https://github.com/conda-forge/julia-feedstock
|__/                   |

julia> using SparseArrays
┌ Warning: CHOLMOD version incompatibility
│
│ Julia was compiled with CHOLMOD version 4.0.4. It is
│ currently linked with version 3.0.14.
│ This might cause Julia to terminate when working with
│ sparse matrix factorizations, e.g. solving systems of
│ equations with \.
│
│ It is recommended that you use Julia with the same major
│ version of CHOLMOD as the one used during the build, or
│ download the generic binaries from www.julialang.org,
│ which ship with the correct versions of all dependencies.
└ @ SparseArrays.CHOLMOD ~/mambaforge/envs/julia-test/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/SparseArrays/src/solvers/cholmod.jl:206
```

When not forcing conda-forge (which I expect to have issues anyway) I get:

```
julia> using SparseArrays
ERROR: InitError: could not load library "@rpath/libspqr.2.dylib"
dlopen(@rpath/libspqr.2.dylib, 0x0001): Library not loaded: @rpath/libtbb.dylib
  Referenced from: <D2B2A060-F4A6-34EA-BC95-649F75FE4EA1> /Users/mharradon/mambaforge/envs/julia-test/lib/libspqr.2.0.9.dylib

Stacktrace:

  [2] dlopen

  [4] __init__()
```
